applied_optimizations.name: get_valid_kv_indices triton kernel for CUDA graph compatible KV index extraction
applied_optimizations.description: Added a triton kernel `_get_valid_kv_indices_kernel` and wrapper `get_valid_kv_indices` that replaces the dynamic-shape boolean mask `page_table_1[page_table_1 != -1]` with fixed-shape operations (sum, cumsum, triton scatter). The kernel reads pre-computed CSR indptr offsets and scatters valid entries contiguously. All operations produce fixed-shape tensors, making them CUDA graph capturable.

applied_optimizations.name: Fix _forward_aiter decode to use get_valid_kv_indices and correct mla_decode_fwd call
applied_optimizations.description: Replaced the dynamic boolean mask in _forward_aiter with the new get_valid_kv_indices kernel. Pre-allocated kv_indices_buf and kv_indptr buffers in __init__. Removed the scalar assignment `kv_indptr[0] = 0` which was not allowed during CUDA graph capture (torch.zeros already initializes to 0). Fixed mla_decode_fwd call to use keyword arguments for sm_scale and removed unsupported logit_cap argument.

applied_optimizations.name: Add aiter prefill implementation (_forward_aiter_prefill)
applied_optimizations.description: Added _forward_aiter_prefill method that delegates to the tilelang sparse forward kernel (tilelang_sparse_fwd), which is already AMD GPU compatible. Added 'aiter' to the forward_extend dispatch logic and to _NSA_IMPL_T type alias.

notes: Root cause 1: `page_table_1[page_table_1 != -1]` produces dynamic output shape, breaking CUDA graph capture
Root cause 2: `kv_indptr[0] = 0` scalar assignment not allowed during stream capture on HIP
Root cause 3: mla_decode_fwd positional args had type mismatch (float passed as page_size int)
Fix: Pre-compute indptr with fixed-shape ops (sum+cumsum), scatter with triton kernel using pre-computed offsets
The aiter prefill implementation reuses the existing tilelang sparse kernel which works on AMD GPUs
SCORE: 100 achieved - server starts with cudagraph capture and responds correctly
